phase_name: Stale Workspace Intelligence and RC Closure

completed_tasks.name: Preserve authoritative catalogs larger than 16 workspaces while retaining the 16-workspace creation admission policy
Fix OpenTUI preload, orphan-text startup, renderer ownership, and fatal terminal cleanup
Prevent superseded service authentication from repopulating closed client access
Repeat Plan 127-12 regression and exact-candidate freeze after post-freeze fixes
completed_tasks.progress: Focused catalog, TUI PTY, authentication shutdown, Vitest, TUI, build, package typecheck, architecture, and secure-frame checks are green. The complete post-fix regression and a replacement exact-SHA freeze have not been run.

remaining_tasks.name: Reproduce the web auto-launch environment-refresh failure from a fresh build and determine whether it remains actionable
Run the complete post-fix regression: npm test, npm run typecheck, npm run coverage, and npm run release:check without --tag
Freeze a new clean exact candidate SHA and supersede all f7bdca75 receipt, validation, summary, and state references
Obtain Plan 127-13 exact-SHA hosted/authenticated and physical/manual evidence
Run Plan 127-14 reconciliation only after both Plan 127-13 checkpoints pass
Complete milestone audit, completion, and cleanup

blockers.description: The recorded candidate f7bdca75f2545664251e88b233693b67fd37ee5c predates three runtime fixes and is superseded as the intended RC candidate.
blockers.workaround: Run the full regression on a clean post-handoff tree, freeze a replacement SHA, and reset every exact-SHA external/manual row to PENDING for that replacement.

blockers.description: Plan 127-13 hosted/authenticated and physical/manual evidence remains unavailable by explicit user choice.
blockers.workaround: Keep every external/manual receipt PENDING and do not create 127-13-SUMMARY.md or start Plan 127-14 until complete safe evidence exists for the replacement candidate.

blockers.description: A first web launch returned LocalEnvironmentPreparationError until the service was started separately; the later snapshot failure was fixed, but the initial auto-launch observation has not been reproduced from a fresh build.
blockers.workaround: Rebuild, ensure no managed service is running, retry node packages/cli/dist/index.js web, and investigate the actual launch path if it still fails. Do not blame Node 26 without evidence.
Do not tag, push, publish packages, create a GitHub Release, or dispatch release-only workflows. Separate explicit authorization is required for any such action.

blocking_constraints.name: flat-agent-cap
blocking_constraints.description: The prior 'up to 5 agents' allowance was violated by recursive fan-out that created hundreds of agents and the user stopped it.
blocking_constraints.mitigation: Resume solo. Do not launch an agent or workflow unless the user explicitly authorizes it again. Any future numeric cap counts all nested and recursively spawned work, not only top-level calls.

blocking_constraints.name: exact-sha-evidence-reset
blocking_constraints.description: Runtime defects were found after the old candidate freeze, so old exact-SHA deterministic and human evidence cannot approve the repaired source.
blocking_constraints.mitigation: Complete a fresh full regression, freeze a new clean SHA, mark the old candidate superseded, and keep all hosted/authenticated/manual evidence PENDING for the new SHA.

blocking_constraints.name: release-authority-boundary
blocking_constraints.description: Feature and candidate validation do not authorize an outward release action.
blocking_constraints.mitigation: Never tag, push, publish, create a release, or dispatch a release workflow without a new explicit operator instruction.

blocking_constraints.name: node-floor-not-ceiling
blocking_constraints.description: Node 24 was incorrectly treated as the only supported runtime when the user confirmed Node 26 also works.
blocking_constraints.mitigation: Treat Node 24 as the supported minimum/default validation target, not a maximum; diagnose failures from evidence rather than version assumption.

human_actions_pending.action: Supply complete safe hosted runtime, configured shell/SSH-agent, authenticated GitHub/GitLab status, and forge same/fork/recovery receipts for the replacement exact candidate SHA.
human_actions_pending.context: Plan 127-13 Task 1; the user chose to keep this evidence pending.

human_actions_pending.action: Perform complete live lifecycle, physical browser/xterm, responsive screenshot, interactive OpenTUI, and human cross-client parity validation for the replacement exact candidate SHA.
human_actions_pending.context: Plan 127-13 Task 2; current automated PTY coverage does not substitute for this evidence.

decisions.decision: Keep CLIENT_MODEL_LIMITS.workspaces at 16 only for workspace-creation admission; authoritative active, archived, stale, pin, and priority read/projection sets are not truncated.
decisions.rationale: The 16-row service-client policy must not make existing larger catalogs unreadable or silently incomplete.

decisions.decision: Fix the TUI through correct @opentui/solid preload and explicit CliRenderer ownership rather than papering over false Show branches with fallback nodes.
decisions.rationale: Solid's server runtime emitted an empty raw string, and an internally owned renderer leaked raw/alternate-screen state when mounting failed.

decisions.decision: Use access generations so closeServiceClient supersedes an in-flight authentication request.
decisions.rationale: A late authentication result must not reopen cached access after shutdown.

decisions.decision: Keep all Plan 127-13 external/manual evidence pending and stop before Plan 127-14.
decisions.rationale: The user explicitly selected pending evidence rather than authorizing hosted/authenticated checks or accepting incomplete evidence.

decisions.decision: Treat Node 24 as the supported floor/default test target, not the exclusive runtime.
decisions.rationale: The user confirmed the application also works on Node 26.
No tag, push, package publication, GitHub Release, or release-workflow dispatch has been authorized.

next_action: Read and acknowledge every blocking constraint in .continue-here.md, resume solo, consume the one-shot handoff, reproduce the web auto-launch observation from a fresh build, then run the complete regression before freezing a replacement exact candidate. Do not start Plan 127-13 or any outward release action.
context_notes: Phase 127 Plans 127-01 through 127-12 are implemented, but the old candidate ledger is obsolete after commits 42d92518, e5a59502, and 9d319c32. Before this handoff the branch planning/phase-127-revision-1 was clean at 9d319c3243b594d3a3d71c15a51234099419a8e7. STATE.md, 127-RECEIPTS.md, 127-VALIDATION.md, and 127-12-SUMMARY.md still name f7bdca75f2545664251e88b233693b67fd37ee5c and must not be treated as current approval. No 127-13-SUMMARY.md or 127-14-SUMMARY.md exists. No managed service, web, or TUI process and no async-job manifest was observed at pause. Placeholder scans of existing Phase 127 summaries found only narrative statements saying no placeholders were introduced, not incomplete summary content. Focused post-fix gates are green, but npm test, full typecheck, coverage, and release:check still need one final run on the replacement candidate source.
